exercises.title: 4-7-8 Breathing Challenge
exercises.description: Practice the 4-7-8 breathing technique twice daily for 7 days (morning and before bed). Track your stress level before and after each session (1-10 scale). Record how long it takes to fall asleep on practice nights vs. non-practice nights.
hints: Start with 2 cycles and work up to 4 — the hold may be challenging at first
Place your tongue tip behind your upper front teeth as instructed
Be consistent — the benefits compound over days, not minutes

solution.code:
4-7-8 BREATHING TRACKING LOG:

| Day | Time    | Cycles | Before (1-10) | After (1-10) | Change | Sleep time |
|-----|---------|--------|---------------|--------------|--------|------------|
| Mon | 7 AM    | 2      | 5             | 3            | -2     | -          |
| Mon | 10 PM   | 2      | 6             | 4            | -2     | 12 min     |
| Tue | 7 AM    | 3      | 4             | 2            | -2     | -          |
| Tue | 10 PM   | 3      | 5             | 3            | -2     | 10 min     |
| Wed | 7 AM    | 3      | 5             | 3            | -2     | -          |
| Wed | 10 PM   | 3      | 7             | 4            | -3     | 8 min      |
| Thu | 7 AM    | 4      | 4             | 2            | -2     | -          |
| Thu | 10 PM   | 4      | 6             | 3            | -3     | 7 min      |
| Fri | 7 AM    | 4      | 5             | 3            | -2     | -          |
| Fri | 10 PM   | 4      | 5             | 2            | -3     | 9 min      |
| Sat | 7 AM    | 4      | 4             | 2            | -2     | -          |
| Sat | 10 PM   | 4      | 6             | 3            | -3     | 8 min      |
| Sun | 7 AM    | 4      | 3             | 1            | -2     | -          |
| Sun | 10 PM   | 4      | 5             | 2            | -3     | 10 min     |

RESULTS:
• Average stress reduction: 2.3 points (1-10 scale)
• Average sleep time (practice nights): 9 min
• Average sleep time (non-practice nights): 18 min
• Best time: Before bed (slightly more effective than morning)
• Conclusion: 4-7-8 significantly reduces stress and improves sleep latency

exercises.title: Progressive Muscle Relaxation Session
exercises.description: Do a full 15-minute PMR session following the body sequence from toes to head. Note which body parts hold the most tension. Then create a 'quick PMR' version (3-5 minutes) that targets only your personal tension hotspots for daily use.
hints: Don't rush — give each muscle group the full 5 seconds of tension + 15 seconds of release
The most common tension spots for developers: jaw, shoulders, hands, and lower back
Practice the quick version at your desk during a stressful day

solution.code:
PMR SESSION LOG:

FULL SESSION (15 min):
• Feet: Low tension
• Calves: Low tension
• Thighs: Low tension  
• Glutes: Medium tension
• Stomach: Low tension
• Hands: HIGH tension (clenched from typing)
• Forearms: HIGH tension (keyboard strain)
• Biceps: Medium tension
• Shoulders: VERY HIGH tension (desk posture)
• Neck: HIGH tension
• Jaw: HIGH tension (realized I was clenching)
• Eyes: Medium tension
• Forehead: Low tension

PERSONAL HOTSPOTS: Hands, Shoulders, Jaw

QUICK PMR VERSION (3 min, for desk use):
1. Hands: Make fists for 5 sec → release for 15 sec
2. Shoulders: Shrug to ears for 5 sec → drop for 15 sec  
3. Jaw: Clench teeth for 5 sec → unclench for 15 sec
4. Take 3 deep breaths
5. Notice the difference

USAGE: Do quick PMR every 2 hours during intense coding sessions

exercises.title: Build a Breathing Habit
exercises.description: Choose one breathing technique and practice it daily for 2 weeks using habit stacking. Attach it to an existing routine (morning coffee, lunch break, bedtime). Track completion and stress reduction daily.
hints: Start with the simplest technique — Box Breathing or Physiological Sigh
Use habit stacking: 'After I [existing habit], I will [breathing technique]'
Track both completion AND effectiveness — a habit that doesn't work isn't worth keeping

solution.code:
BREATHING HABIT DESIGN:

CHOSEN TECHNIQUE: Box Breathing (4-4-4-4)
HABIT STACK: After I pour my morning coffee, I will do 4 cycles of box breathing
WHEN: 7:30 AM daily
WHERE: Kitchen, while coffee cools
REMINDER: Sticky note on coffee maker

TRACKING (2 weeks):
| Day | Done? | Stress Before | Stress After | Notes |
|-----|-------|---------------|--------------|-------|
| Mon | ✓     | 5             | 3            | Felt calmer all morning |
| Tue | ✓     | 4             | 2            | Easy day, helped maintain |
| Wed | ✓     | 6             | 3            | Stressful day, very helpful |
| Thu | ✓     | 5             | 3            | Good focus after |
| Fri | ✓     | 4             | 2            | End of week, nice ritual |
| Sat | ✓     | 3             | 1            | Weekend, less stressful |
| Sun | ✓     | 3             | 2            | Prep for week ahead |
| Mon | ✓     | 5             | 3            | Second week, more natural |
| Tue | ✓     | 6             | 3            | Production issue, helped |
| Wed | ✓     | 5             | 2            | Getting automatic |
| Thu | ✓     | 7             | 4            | High stress day, essential |
| Fri | ✓     | 4             | 2            | Week complete! |
| Sat | ✓     | 3             | 2            | Habit established |
| Sun | ✓     | 3             | 1            | 14/14 days! |

RESULTS:
• Completion rate: 100% (14/14)
• Average stress reduction: 2.4 points
• Best finding: Habit is now automatic — no motivation needed
• Next: Add evening 4-7-8 breathing for sleep
